fresh_facts.claim: Approximately 2,200-2,500 US Marines from the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit were ordered toward the Middle East near the Strait of Hormuz aboard amphibious ships including USS Tripoli.
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth approved the deployment of 2,200-2,500 US Marines toward the Middle East on or before March 14, 2026.
The US struck more than 15,000 targets in Iran over nearly two weeks prior to March 14, 2026, resulting in 13 US personnel killed in the conflict.
Iran escalated missile and drone attacks on US/Israeli targets and commercial shipping near the Strait of Hormuz, causing oil prices to surge above $100 per barrel.
An Iranian ballistic missile strike hit a pro-regime rally in Tehran on March 13, killing at least one person, with top leaders including the president and judiciary chief present.
Approximately 2,000 people are reported dead in Iran from US strikes and related conflict in Lebanon.
The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed due to Iranian missile and drone attacks.
On March 8, 2026, the Assembly of Experts officially appointed Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader following his father's death in a U.S.-Israel strike on February 28.
Mojtaba Khamenei's first official message on March 12 emphasized continuation of the war and threatened to close the Strait of Hormuz.
An elite counterterrorism unit (NOPO) was deployed to protect the new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, representing a significant shift from its previous hostage rescue role.
On March 15, Israeli officials stated that two senior intelligence figures—Abdollah Jalali-Nasab and Amir Shariat—linked to the Khatam al-Anbiya emergency command were killed in a Tehran strike.
Iranian state media confirmed the death of Abdollah Jalali-Nasab in a March 15 Tehran strike.
Armed Forces General Staff Basij chief Asadollah Badfar was killed in previous airstrikes, confirmed by anti-regime media on March 10.
Drone strikes on Basij and IRGC-linked checkpoints have resulted in rising losses among forces deployed to control movement in Tehran and other cities.
The Shiraz Revolutionary Court chief stopped appearing at the courthouse and shifted to handling detainee cases from Adelabad prison from within the facility due to security concerns.
Residents of Tehran's Saadat Abad neighborhood reported anti-IRGC night chants including 'death to the IRGC' despite regime expansion of patrols and checkpoints.
The IRGC Intelligence Organization acknowledged on March 12 that 'enemies of the Islamic Republic' are attempting to create fear and encourage street protests.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi denied targeting civilian areas in neighboring states and proposed a 'joint investigative committee' with regional governments on March 15.
Hamas publicly urged Iran not to target neighboring Arab countries while affirming Iran's right to self-defense, breaking with Tehran's wartime posture.
The EU approved sanctions on March 11 against 19 Iranian officials and entities over human rights violations.
EU Commissioner Kallas stated that 'the EU will protect its interests and pursue those responsible for domestic repression' regarding Iran.
On March 13, the UN Fact-Finding Mission on Iran warned that 'civilians were trapped between armed conflict and repression at levels that may amount to crimes against humanity.'
Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama announced plans to invite parliament to vote on declaring Iran a state sponsor of terrorism.
NATO intercepted an Iranian ballistic missile strike on a US air base in southern Turkey on Friday, March 13.

regime_fall.current_status: Unprecedented military escalation continues with 15,000+ US strikes, 7,600 Israeli airstrikes, and 2,200-2,500 additional US Marines deployed to the Strait of Hormuz, while senior Iranian military and intelligence figures are systematically eliminated and Mojtaba Khamenei consolidates power amid internal security concerns, street protests, and effective closure of global shipping lanes. International condemnation intensifies with EU sanctions, UN warnings of crimes against humanity, and NATO interception of Iranian ballistic missiles.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israeli officials confirmed killing of two senior intelligence figures (Abdollah Jalali-Nasab and Amir Shariat) on March 15 in Tehran strikes, while Iranian state media confirmed Jalali-Nasab's death, signaling continued decapitation of regime command structure.
